data.label: Patient Record
data.description:
A person's medical record in the Cordova healthcare system.
Business Context: Links to Civil Registry via National ID. All clinical cells carry Restricted:PatientCare policy.
Purpose: Comprehensive medical record for a Cordova resident

items.label: National ID (CID)
items.description: Cordova National Identity Number. Format: COR-PPCC-NNNNNN where PP is the two-letter province code (AL=Aldara, BR=Brevina, CE=Celara), CC is the two-digit city code (01-03), and NNNNNN is a six-digit sequence number. Issued at birth or naturalization. Required for all government transactions.

items.label: Medical Record Number
items.description: Institution-specific medical record number for linking to clinical records

items.label: Allergies and Conditions
items.description:
Known allergies and chronic conditions.
Purpose: Document allergies and ongoing medical conditions for clinical decision-making
Parent Cluster: Patient Record

items.label: Allergy Description
items.description: Description of a known allergy (drug, food, environmental)

items.label: Chronic Condition
items.description: Name or description of a chronic medical condition (e.g., hypertension, diabetes, asthma)

items.label: Condition Status
items.description: Current status of the condition

items.label: Severity (3-Point)
items.description: Three-point severity scale for clinical findings

items.label: Onset Date
items.description: Date of symptom or disease onset

items.label: Medications
items.description:
Current medication list.
Purpose: Track active medications for drug interaction checking and care coordination
Parent Cluster: Patient Record

items.label: Dosage
items.description: Prescribed dosage (e.g., "500mg", "10mg/mL")

items.label: Medication Name
items.description: Name of the prescribed medication (generic or brand name)

items.label: Frequency (5-Point)
items.description: Five-point frequency scale for symptom or behavior occurrence

items.label: Medication Dosage Amount
items.description: Prescribed dosage amount of the medication in milligrams. Represents the per-administration dose as ordered by the prescribing physician.

items.label: Prescription Date
items.description: Date the medication was prescribed

items.label: Vaccination History
items.description:
Immunization records.
Purpose: Track vaccinations for public health compliance and clinical reference
Parent Cluster: Patient Record

items.label: Lot Number
items.description: Vaccine lot number for traceability

items.label: Vaccine Name
items.description: Name of the vaccine administered

items.label: Vaccine Date
items.description: Date the vaccine was administered

items.label: Visit Record
items.description:
An encounter at a healthcare facility.
Business Context: Visit records link to the Contagion scenario — Carlos Mendoza's admission to Porto Sereno General Hospital
Purpose: Document each clinical encounter with date, provider, reason, diagnosis, and outcome
Parent Cluster: Patient Record

items.label: Diagnosis
items.description: Clinical diagnosis resulting from this visit. This cell carries Restricted:PatientCare policy — it cannot be composed into public reports.

items.label: Facility
items.description: Healthcare facility where the visit occurred

items.label: Reason for Visit
items.description: Chief complaint or reason for the clinical encounter

items.label: Clinical Impression
items.description: Overall clinical impression from neurological examination

items.label: Outcome
items.description: Outcome of the visit

items.label: Visit Type
items.description: Type of clinical visit or encounter

items.label: Body Temperature
items.description: Patient body temperature measured in degrees Celsius during a clinical visit. Normal adult range is 36.1–37.2°C. Triage screening vital sign.

items.label: Diastolic Blood Pressure
items.description: Minimum arterial pressure during cardiac relaxation, measured in mmHg. Normal adult range is 60–80 mmHg. Values ≥90 mmHg indicate hypertension per WHO/AHA guidelines.

items.label: Patient Height
items.description: Patient standing height in centimeters measured during a clinical visit. Used for BMI computation and growth tracking.

items.label: Patient Weight
items.description: Patient body weight in kilograms measured during a clinical visit. Used for medication dosing calculations, BMI computation, and tracking health trends.

items.label: Systolic Blood Pressure
items.description: Peak arterial pressure during cardiac contraction, measured in mmHg. Normal adult range is 90–120 mmHg. Values ≥140 mmHg indicate hypertension per WHO/AHA guidelines.

items.label: Visit Date
items.description: Date of clinical visit

subject.label: Primary Care Assignment
subject.description: Patient's assigned primary care provider (clinician) and primary care facility. W3C PROV-DM Agent supporting Person and Organization.

provider.label: Healthcare Provider
provider.description: Healthcare provider (facility and/or clinician) delivering care. W3C PROV-DM Agent supporting both Person and Organization types.
